data.title: Analysis: The IPOs of SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic may attract significant funds and impact liquidity in the BTC and crypto markets.
data.description: SpaceX secretly filed its S-1 document with the U.S. SEC earlier this month, planning an IPO with a valuation of approximately $1.75 trillion and a fundraising target of $75 billion. If it lists as expected in June, it will surpass Saudi Aramco's $29 billion fundraising record in 2019 to become the largest IPO in history. Meanwhile, OpenAI and Anthropic also plan to list in the second half of this year, with the three companies expected to attract a combined total of over $240 billion. Market analysts believe that these mega-IPOs could significantly drain liquidity from risk assets, and the crypto market is currently in the same pool of funds. Because mainstream crypto assets such as Bitcoin and Ethereum have been highly correlated with risk appetite on Nasdaq and the U.S. stock market in recent years, a large amount of capital has shifted to subscribing to tech giant IPOs, potentially weakening buying support for BTC, ETH, and altcoins. Analysts point out that Coinbase's IPO in 2021 coincided with a temporary peak in Bitcoin's price, and the SpaceX IPO may once again become a significant liquidity turning point in the crypto market. (CoinDesk)

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Chinese Gold Jewelry Prices Remain Stable
data.description: On April 25, Jin10 reported that the prices of gold jewelry in China have remained largely unchanged compared to the previous day. According to Jin10, several brands are quoting prices within the range of 1,440 yuan per gram to 1,445 yuan per gram.

data.title: Avraham Eisenberg's Wallet Activity Resurfaces Post-Mango Markets Exploit
data.description: Avraham Eisenberg's wallet has reportedly signed a new transaction following the 2022 Mango Markets exploit, which was valued at approximately $110 million. According to NS3.AI, this development comes after U.S. prosecutors appealed a not-guilty verdict against Eisenberg in January.

data.title: Planet Midday News
data.description: 1. Only one person was approved for Trump's "Gold Card" visa program, a stark contrast to the expected $100 billion; 2. Pump.fun has repurchased over 35% of its total circulating supply, with a cumulative repurchase value exceeding $360 million; 3. Nvidia's market capitalization has once again surpassed $5 trillion; 4. A US soldier who illegally profited $400,000 on Polymarket was refused an account by Kalshi; 5. Financial reports revealed that Trump conducted dozens of bond transactions in March, totaling millions of dollars; 6. Bitdeer maintains zero holdings, selling 185.7 BTC this week; 7. Bitcoin developer Paul Sztorc plans to launch the hard fork network eCash in August, supporting 1:1 BTC exchange, sparking community controversy; 8. The address of Mango Markets attacker Avi Eisenberg reappeared with on-chain signatures, drawing community attention; 9. An early Ethereum whale transferred 10,000 ETH to a multi-signature address, which may be sold soon; 10. An address associated with the Balancer attacker transferred 5,609 ETH, worth $13 million, to THORChain in the past 9 hours.

data.title: The Balancer attacker's associated address transferred 5,609 ETH, worth $13 million, to THORChain in the past 9 hours.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the address associated with the Balancer attacker transferred 5,609 ETH, worth $13 million, to THORChain in the past 9 hours. In November 2025, Balancer suffered a theft of over $116 million, and the suspects in the Aave attack are the same North Korean hacking group, Lazarus Group. Both attacks have recently been frequently using Tornado attacks for money laundering.

data.title: Balancer Attack Linked to North Korean Hackers, Funds Moved to THORChain
data.description: Analyst @ai_9684xtpa posted on X that hackers have been actively laundering funds, with the Balancer attacker transferring 5,609 ETH, valued at $13 million, to THORChain over the past nine hours. The Balancer platform suffered a theft exceeding $116 million, with suspicions pointing to the same group involved in the Aave attack, identified as the North Korean hacker organization Lazarus Group. Both incidents have seen frequent use of Tornado Cash for laundering activities.

data.title: An early Ethereum whale transferred 10,000 ETH to a multisignature address and may sell it soon.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, an early Ethereum whale has transferred another 10,000 ETH (approximately $23.21 million) to a multi-signature address, which may be sold soon. The address receiving the tokens, 0x26c…B9392, has deposited a total of 12,001 ETH into OKX over the past two months, worth a total of $24.62 million. Based on past experience, ETH transferred to this address will subsequently flow to exchanges in batches.

data.title: Mango Markets attacker Avi Eisenberg's address reappearing with on-chain signatures has drawn community attention.
data.description: Arkham posted on the X platform that Avi Eisenberg, the attacker who profited approximately $110 million in 2022 by manipulating the Mango Markets price oracle, has recently had his on-chain address signing transactions again, drawing widespread attention from the community. Avi Eisenberg previously exploited vulnerabilities in Mango Finance to execute "high-profit trades," subsequently threatening to attack Aave, and was also arrested and imprisoned after attempting to liquidate others' positions on Curve, which resulted in his own liquidation. Now that his address has reappeared on-chain activity, the market is speculating whether he has returned to the crypto market, although no further details have been disclosed yet.

data.title: Advancements in Model Training Techniques
data.description: Recent developments in model training focus on enhancing capabilities through compression, abstraction, and learning. According to PANews, these advancements aim to strengthen models post-deployment, allowing them to improve and adapt over time. The emphasis is on refining the processes that enable models to become more efficient and effective in their operations.

data.title: ZetaChain Integrates OpenAI's GPT-5.5 into Anuma
data.description: ZetaChain announced on April 25 the integration of OpenAI's GPT-5.5 into its platform, Anuma. According to NS3.AI, this integration allows the model to maintain context for over one million tokens, enhancing user experience. Users can access GPT-5.5 on Anuma without requiring a separate installation. This development follows the recent addition of Alibaba's Qwen 3.6 Max to the platform.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $14.4489 million yesterday, marking the ninth consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $14.4489 million yesterday (April 24th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF (IBIT) saw the largest single-day net inflow of $22.879 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $190 million. This was followed by the Morgan Stanley ETF (MSBT) with a net inflow of $11.1294 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $153 million. Conversely, the Ark Invest and 21Shares ETF (ARKB) experienced the largest single-day net outflow of $9.016 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.61 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $102.637 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) is 6.6%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $58.564 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $23.3807 million yesterday, with only BlackRock's ETHB showing net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $23.3807 million yesterday (April 24th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the BlackRock Stalled ETH ETF (ETHB), with a net inflow of $32.252 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $32.252 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the BlackRock ETF (ETHA), with a net outflow of $7.714 million, bringing its historical total net outflow to $28.6641 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs was $13.791 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total Ethereum market capitalization) of 4.91%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $12.099 billion.

data.title: A whale spent 200 ETH to buy 1.72 billion ASTEROIDs in the past 7 hours.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale named "0x2D2D" spent 200 ETH (US$463,500) to buy 1.72 billion ASTEROID in the past 7 hours. The whale currently holds a total of 8.38 billion ASTEROID, worth US$2.27 million.

data.title: Significant WLD Transfer to CEX Raises Speculation of WorldCoin Team Involvement
data.description:
On April 25, a substantial transfer of 21,177,000 WLD tokens was made to a centralized exchange (CEX), raising speculation about potential involvement from the WorldCoin team. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, two wallet addresses were responsible for the transfer, which is estimated to be worth millions of dollars.

The wallet addresses involved in the transaction are 0x97db11b279B14201324AaB547D5fe486934CC75 and 0x6c94e4FE289C70d60bA5F4e50bebd5b96C933d35. The movement of such a large amount of WLD tokens has led to discussions within the cryptocurrency community about the intentions behind the transfer and its implications for WorldCoin.

While the identities behind the wallet addresses remain unknown, the transaction has sparked interest and speculation regarding the future activities of WorldCoin and its team. The transfer to a CEX suggests potential strategic moves or operational decisions by the team, although no official statement has been released to confirm these assumptions.

data.title: Crypto Startups Secure $37 Million Amid AI Fundraising Challenges
data.description: Crypto startups have successfully raised nearly $37 million this week, despite challenges posed by AI startups in the fundraising landscape. According to NS3.AI, industry expert Paul Brody noted that the rise of AI startups is complicating the fundraising efforts for crypto ventures. DefiLlama data highlighted significant deals, including a $10 million funding round for BetHog and $8 million raises for both Hata and KAIO, marking them as some of the largest transactions of the week.

data.title: Hong Kong media: Former Huobi senior manager acquitted in case involving the transfer of nearly 5 million USDT
data.description: Chen Bailiang, a former senior manager at Huobi Group, was accused of using an alias to open a personal account on the platform and illegally conducting cryptocurrency "dark pool trading" with no price or quantity restrictions. He was also accused of transferring approximately 4.999 million USDT (about HK$39.18 million) from his account to his accounts on OKX and Binance, involving seven charges including dishonest access to a computer and money laundering. After deliberation, a Hong Kong High Court jury acquitted him on all charges on April 24. The prosecution alleged that he used the identity "Chen Feng" to complete real-name authentication in 2020 and used this account to conduct dark pool trading with company accounts, but the jury ultimately did not accept these charges, and the court granted the defendant's application for legal costs. (HK01)

data.title: Bitcoin developer Paul Sztorc plans to launch a hard fork network, eCash, in August, supporting a 1:1 exchange rate with BTC, sparking controversy in the community.
data.description: Bitcoin developer Paul Sztorc announced that the Bitcoin hard fork network eCash will officially launch this August. BTC holders can exchange their BTC for eCash at a 1:1 ratio after the hard fork goes live. The network's Layer 1 node software will reportedly be a "near-copy" of the Bitcoin Core client, continuing to use the SHA-256 hash algorithm and lowering the initial mining difficulty to attract more miners. Simultaneously, eCash will also feature seven Layer 2 scaling networks called "drivechains" to improve transaction throughput and support optional on-chain privacy features. Paul Sztorc stated that eCash differs from Bitcoin Cash in 2017, no longer using the "Bitcoin" name, and claims it is a long-term solution to Bitcoin's scalability and privacy issues. However, his proposed plan to manually redistribute a portion of Satoshi Nakamoto's approximately 1.1 million BTC to early investors has sparked strong controversy in the community, with some Bitcoin supporters criticizing the move as "theft" and a violation of Bitcoin principles. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Bitdeer Reports Zero Bitcoin Holdings After Recent Sales
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company Bitdeer has released its latest Bitcoin holdings data on the X platform. According to Odaily, for the week ending April 24, Bitdeer mined 185.7 BTC and sold an equivalent amount, resulting in a net addition of zero BTC. The company continues to maintain zero Bitcoin holdings.

data.title: Bitdeer maintains zero holdings and sold 185.7 BTC this week.
data.description: Bitdeer, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company, released its latest Bitcoin holdings data on the X platform. As of the week ending April 24, its Bitcoin mining output was 185.7 BTC, but it sold 185.7 BTC during the same period, resulting in a net increase of 0 BTC. It currently maintains a zero Bitcoin holding.

data.title: XChat Launches on App Store with Enhanced Privacy Features
data.description: XChat, a messaging application developed by X, is now available for download on the App Store. According to Foresight News, users can install the app and log in using their X account without needing a phone number or email address. The app features end-to-end encryption, ensuring that messages are visible only to the sender and recipient, with the platform unable to access them. Additionally, XChat supports self-destructing messages and allows for the transfer of any type of file.
